label: 4552 Definitions
notes: Termination of SectionFor termination of section, see .section 4564(a) of this title

children.heading: Critical component
children.label: 1 Critical component
children.text: The term “critical component” includes such components, subsystems, systems, and related special tooling and test equipment essential to the production, repair, maintenance, or operation of weapon systems or other items of equipment identified by the President as being essential to the execution of the national security strategy of the United States. Components identified as critical by a National Security Assessment conducted pursuant to or by a Presidential determination as a result of a petition filed under shall be designated as critical components for purposes of this chapter, unless the President determines that the designation is unwarranted.section 113(i) of title 10section 1862 of title 19

children.heading: Critical infrastructure
children.label: 2 Critical infrastructure
children.text: The term “critical infrastructure” means any systems and assets, whether physical or cyber-based, so vital to the United States that the degradation or destruction of such systems and assets would have a debilitating impact on national security, including, but not limited to, national economic security and national public health or safety.

children.heading: Critical technology
children.label: 3 Critical technology
children.text: The term “critical technology” includes any technology designated by the President to be essential to the national defense.

children.heading: Critical technology item
children.label: 4 Critical technology item
children.text: The term “critical technology item” means materials directly employing, derived from, or utilizing a critical technology.

children.label: A
children.text: to furnish materials, industrial resources, or a critical technology for the national defense; or

children.label: B
children.text: to perform services for the national defense.

children.heading: Domestic industrial base
children.label: 6 Domestic industrial base
children.text: The term “domestic industrial base” means domestic sources which are providing, or which would be reasonably expected to provide, materials or services to meet national defense requirements during peacetime, national emergency, or war.

children.label: i
children.text: that performs in the United States or Canada substantially all of the research and development, engineering, manufacturing, and production activities required of such business concern under a contract with the United States relating to a critical component or a critical technology item; and

children.label: ii
children.text: that procures from business concerns described in clause (i) substantially all of any components and assemblies required under a contract with the United States relating to a critical component or critical technology item.

children.heading: Domestic source for subchapter II
children.label: B Domestic source for subchapter II
aa
children.text: the United States or Canada; or

children.label: bb
children.text: subject to clause (ii), Australia or the United Kingdom; and

children.label: II
children.text: procures from business concerns described in subclause (I) substantially all of any components or assemblies required under a contract with the United States relating to a critical component or critical technology item.

children.heading: Limitations on use of business concerns in Australia and United Kingdom
children.label: ii Limitations on use of business concerns in Australia and United Kingdom

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: A business concern described in clause (i)(I)(bb) may be treated as a domestic source only for purposes of the exercise of authorities under subchapter II relating to national defense matters that cannot be fully addressed with business concerns described in clause (i)(I)(aa).

children.label: aa
children.text: a defense article, as defined in ; orsection 301 of title 10

children.label: bb
children.text: materials critical to national security, as defined in .section 98h–1(f) of this title

children.heading: Facilities
children.label: 8 Facilities
children.text: The term “facilities” includes all types of buildings, structures, or other improvements to real property (but excluding farms, churches or other places of worship, and private dwelling houses), and services relating to the use of any such building, structure, or other improvement.

children.heading: Foreign source
children.label: 9 Foreign source
children.text: The term “foreign source” means a business entity other than a “domestic source”.

children.heading: Guaranteeing agency
children.label: 10 Guaranteeing agency
children.text: The term “guaranteeing agency” means a department or agency of the United States engaged in procurement for the national defense.

children.label: A
children.text: to prevent terrorist attacks within the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: to reduce the vulnerability of the United States to terrorism;

children.label: C
children.text: to minimize damage from a terrorist attack in the United States; and

children.label: D
children.text: to recover from a terrorist attack in the United States.

children.heading: Industrial resources
children.label: 12 Industrial resources
children.text: The term “industrial resources” means materials, services, processes, or manufacturing equipment (including the processes, technologies, and ancillary services for the use of such equipment) needed to establish or maintain an efficient and modern national defense industrial base.

children.label: A
children.text: any raw materials (including minerals, metals, and advanced processed materials), commodities, articles, components (including critical components), products, and items of supply; and

children.label: B
children.text: any technical information or services ancillary to the use of any such materials, commodities, articles, components, products, or items.

children.heading: National defense
children.label: 14 National defense
children.text: The term “national defense” means programs for military and energy production or construction, military or critical infrastructure assistance to any foreign nation, homeland security, stockpiling, space, and any directly related activity. Such term includes emergency preparedness activities conducted pursuant to title VI of The Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act [ et seq.] and critical infrastructure protection and restoration.42 U.S.C. 5195

children.heading: Person
children.label: 15 Person
children.text: The term “person” includes an individual, corporation, partnership, association, or any other organized group of persons, or legal successor or representative thereof, or any State or local government or agency thereof.

children.label: A
children.text: the development, production, processing, distribution, delivery, or use of an industrial resource or a critical technology item;

children.label: B
children.text: the construction of facilities;

children.label: C
children.text: the movement of individuals and property by all modes of civil transportation; or

children.label: D
children.text: other national defense programs and activities.

children.heading: Small business concern
children.label: 17 Small business concern
children.text: The term “small business concern” means a business concern that meets the requirements of and the regulations promulgated pursuant to that section, and includes such business concerns owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals or by women.section 632(a) of title 15
